about.title: About PDF Toolkit
about.description: Learn what PDF Toolkit does, how it is operated, and who it is designed for.
about.body:
What the Service Provides

The public website gives users a clean interface for common PDF workflows. Each tool is designed to keep the task simple, direct, and understandable without requiring a long setup process.

How Processing Works

Document processing is handled by the backend service after a user submits a job. Files are retained only for the operational time needed to finish the requested action and are removed by cleanup routines after expiry.

Who It Is For

PDF Toolkit is intended for freelancers, students, office teams, and anyone who needs dependable document utilities in a browser-based workflow.

ad-disclosure.title: Advertising Disclosure
ad-disclosure.description: Understand how promotional placements and advertising-enabled areas are separated from the core PDF workflow.
ad-disclosure.body:
Where Ads Can Appear

Advertising may appear on content-oriented areas such as the homepage, guides, or other informational sections. Ads should not appear inside upload dropzones, result panels, or primary document action controls.

Runtime Control

Advertising and marketing integrations are resolved at runtime from the CMS so the operator can update approved settings without requiring a full frontend rebuild.

Clear Separation

The site is designed to keep sponsored or promotional placements visually separate from the main tool workflow so users can distinguish product actions from advertising.

contact.title: Contact PDF Toolkit
contact.description: Get in touch for support, business questions, or policy-related requests.
contact.body:
Support Requests

If a tool fails, a page does not load correctly, or a result looks unexpected, contact support and include the page you were using and the approximate time of the issue.

Business and Advertising

The same contact channel may be used for partnership proposals, advertising inquiries, and questions related to site policies or publisher information.

cookies.title: Cookie Notice
cookies.description: Learn about cookies and related browser storage used for runtime behavior, analytics, and advertising controls.
cookies.body:
Essential Use

Essential cookies or equivalent storage may be used to support application behavior, administrative session handling, and security controls.

Analytics and Advertising

If analytics, verification, or advertising integrations are enabled, related identifiers or tags may be loaded on the approved pages configured by the site operator.

Future Controls

As the product evolves, additional consent or disclosure controls may be added where required by the audience, region, or integration in use.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.description: Understand how PDF Toolkit handles uploaded files, operational data, and service-related measurements.
privacy.body:
File Processing

When a user submits a PDF or related file, the file is processed to complete the requested tool action. This can include merging, splitting, compressing, converting, protecting, or watermarking a document.

Temporary Retention and Cleanup

Uploaded files and generated outputs are stored temporarily while the job is active. Cleanup routines remove expired jobs so temporary document data does not remain available indefinitely.

Operational Analytics

The site may collect limited operational signals such as page path, request time, approximate location, device category, browser family, and referrer information to understand service usage and improve reliability.

Questions and Requests

If you need clarification about privacy or data handling, contact the support email listed on the site.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.description: Review the basic usage rules for PDF Toolkit and its document-processing tools.
terms.body:
Acceptable Use

You are responsible for the files you upload and for ensuring you have the right to process them. The service must not be used for unlawful content or abusive activity.

Availability and Limits

The service is provided on an as-available basis. Tool behavior, supported formats, limits, and processing safeguards may change as the platform evolves.

Output Review

Generated files should be reviewed before use in legal, compliance-sensitive, or business-critical workflows. Automated processing is helpful, but it does not replace user review.

faq.question: What is the upload size limit for PDF tools?
faq.answer: By default the toolkit is configured for practical day-to-day usage. If a file is rejected, split large documents first or compress them before retrying.

faq.question: How long are uploaded files kept on the server?
faq.answer: Files are processed for the active request and cleaned by expiry routines. The service is designed for temporary operational storage, not long-term file hosting.

faq.question: How can I ensure merged PDFs keep the correct order?
faq.answer: Rename files before upload and verify page count before export. The merge workflow is most reliable when you set final order once and run a quick QA pass.

faq.question: Will compression reduce document quality?
faq.answer: Compression can lower image quality depending on source and target size. For business-critical documents, validate at 100% zoom before sharing.

faq.question: Can I password protect and then share securely?
faq.answer: Yes. Share the document and password in separate channels, and use clear file naming so recipients know a password is required.

faq.question: Where should I report tool issues or support requests?
faq.answer: Use the Contact page form or the published support email. Include page URL, action attempted, and approximate time so issues can be reproduced quickly.

guides.title: Compress PDF with quality control for text and images
guides.excerpt: How to reduce file size while keeping text readable and images acceptable for web, email, and documentation portals.
guides.body:
Compression is most effective when you choose target quality based on destination.

1. Start from a clean source
If possible, use an export from the source app instead of a scanned print copy.

2. Pick target context first
For email and chat sharing, prioritize smaller size. For policy docs and official records, prioritize readability.

3. Validate at 100 percent zoom
Check headings, tables, and small footnotes after compression.

4. Keep two outputs when needed
Store a lightweight share version and a higher quality archive version.

This approach avoids sending huge files while protecting document quality where it matters.

guides.title: Merge PDFs with a consistent order and naming workflow
guides.excerpt: A practical sequence for collecting files, validating page order, and exporting one clean final PDF without rework.
guides.body:
Use this process when you need one final combined document for submission, sharing, or archive.

1. Collect and label files before upload
Rename each file so order is obvious before you start. This reduces mistakes during merge.

2. Upload in final order and verify total pages
After upload, compare expected page count with the tool summary.

3. Reorder only once
Drag pages into final sequence, then do a quick pass from top to bottom to confirm sections are complete.

4. Export and run a quick QA check
Open the exported PDF and confirm title page, section breaks, and page continuity.

If this document is client-facing, keep a versioned naming pattern such as ProjectName_v1_final.pdf.

guides.title: Protect and share PDF securely without blocking real users
guides.excerpt: A simple security checklist for password usage, sharing channels, and recovery practices when documents are sensitive.
guides.body:
Security should be strong but still usable for recipients.

1. Apply password protection only when needed
Use protection for contracts, personal data, and internal-only documents.

2. Share password and file in separate channels
Send the file by email or portal and send password by a different channel.

3. Use clear internal naming
Mark protected files with a suffix so your team knows password is required.

4. Keep recovery steps documented
Store owner-side recovery instructions in internal documentation so teams are not blocked later.

5. Revoke and regenerate for sensitive updates
If document scope changes, generate a new protected output and replace old copies where possible.
